heading: Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board
label: 7802 Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. Effective Date of 1998 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–206, title I, § 1101(d)112 Stat. 697July 22, 1998“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 4946 and 6103 of this title] shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act [].July 22, 1998“(2).—Initial nominations to internal revenue service oversight boardThe President shall submit the initial nominations under section 7802 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as added by this section, to the Senate not later than 6 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [].July 22, 1998“(3).—Effect on actions prior to appointment of oversight boardNothing in this section shall be construed to invalidate the actions and authority of the Internal Revenue Service prior to the appointment of the members of the Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board.”

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established within the Department of the Treasury the Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board (hereafter in this subchapter referred to as the “Oversight Board”).

children.label: A
children.text: six members shall be individuals who are not otherwise Federal officers or employees and who are appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.label: B
children.text: one member shall be the Secretary of the Treasury or, if the Secretary so designates, the Deputy Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: C
children.text: one member shall be the Commissioner of Internal Revenue.

children.label: D
children.text: one member shall be an individual who is a full-time Federal employee or a representative of employees and who is appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.label: i
children.text: Management of large service organizations.

children.label: ii
children.text: Customer service.

children.label: iii
children.text: Federal tax laws, including tax administration and compliance.

children.label: iv
children.text: Information technology.

children.label: v
children.text: Organization development.

children.label: vi
children.text: The needs and concerns of taxpayers.

children.label: vii
children.text: The needs and concerns of small businesses.

children.label: i
children.text: two members shall be appointed for a term of 3 years,

children.label: ii
children.text: two members shall be appointed for a term of 4 years, and

children.label: iii
children.text: two members shall be appointed for a term of 5 years.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: C Reappointment
children.text: An individual who is described in subparagraph (A) or (D) of paragraph (1) may be appointed to no more than two 5-year terms on the Oversight Board.

children.heading: Vacancy
children.label: D Vacancy
children.text: Any vacancy on the Oversight Board shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment. Any member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring before the expiration of the term for which the member’s predecessor was appointed shall be appointed for the remainder of that term.

children.heading: Financial disclosure
children.label: A Financial disclosure
children.text: During the entire period that an individual appointed under subparagraph (A) or (D) of paragraph (1) is a member of the Oversight Board, such individual shall be treated as serving as an officer or employee referred to in , United States Code, for purposes of subchapter I of chapter 131 of such title, except that section 13103(d) of such title shall apply without regard to the number of days of service in the position.section 13103(f) of title 5

children.heading: Restrictions on post-employment
children.label: B Restrictions on post-employment
children.text: For purposes of , United States Code, an individual appointed under subparagraph (A) or (D) of paragraph (1) shall be treated as an employee referred to in section 207(c)(2)(A)(i) of such title during the entire period the individual is a member of the Board, except that subsections (c)(2)(B) and (f) of section 207 of such title shall not apply.section 207(c) of title 18

children.heading: Members who are special Government employees
children.label: C Members who are special Government employees
I
children.text: the Oversight Board or the Internal Revenue Service on any matter;

children.label: II
children.text: the Department of the Treasury on any matter involving the internal revenue laws or involving the management or operations of the Internal Revenue Service; or

children.label: III
children.text: the Department of Justice with respect to litigation involving a matter described in subclause (I) or (II).

children.heading: Compensation for services provided by another
children.label: ii Compensation for services provided by another
I
children.text: such individual shall not be subject to the restrictions of subsection (a)(1) thereof for sharing in compensation earned by another for representations on matters covered by such section, and

children.label: II
children.text: a person shall not be subject to the restrictions of subsection (a)(2) thereof for sharing such compensation with such individual.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: D Waiver
children.text: The President may, only at the time the President nominates the member of the Oversight Board described in paragraph (1)(D), waive for the term of the member any appropriate provision of chapter 11 of title 18, United States Code, to the extent such waiver is necessary to allow such member to participate in the decisions of the Board while continuing to serve as a full-time Federal employee or a representative of employees. Any such waiver shall not be effective unless a written intent of waiver to exempt such member (and actual waiver language) is submitted to the Senate with the nomination of such member.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 4 Quorum
children.text: Five members of the Oversight Board shall constitute a quorum. A majority of members present and voting shall be required for the Oversight Board to take action.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any member of the Oversight Board appointed under subparagraph (A) or (D) of paragraph (1) may be removed at the will of the President.

children.heading: Secretary and Commissioner
children.label: B Secretary and Commissioner
children.text: An individual described in subparagraph (B) or (C) of paragraph (1) shall be removed upon termination of service in the office described in such subparagraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Members of the Oversight Board who are described in subparagraph (A) or (D) of paragraph (1) shall have no personal liability under Federal law with respect to any claim arising out of or resulting from an act or omission by such member within the scope of service as a member.

children.label: i
children.text: to affect any other immunities and protections that may be available to such member under applicable law with respect to such transactions;

children.label: ii
children.text: to affect any other right or remedy against the United States under applicable law; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to limit or alter in any way the immunities that are available under applicable law for Federal officers and employees.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Oversight Board shall oversee the Internal Revenue Service in its administration, management, conduct, direction, and supervision of the execution and application of the internal revenue laws or related statutes and tax conventions to which the United States is a party.

children.heading: Mission of IRS
children.label: B Mission of IRS
children.text: As part of its oversight functions described in subparagraph (A), the Oversight Board shall ensure that the organization and operation of the Internal Revenue Service allows it to carry out its mission.

children.heading: Confidentiality
children.label: C Confidentiality
children.text: The Oversight Board shall ensure that appropriate confidentiality is maintained in the exercise of its duties.

children.label: A
children.text: the development and formulation of Federal tax policy relating to existing or proposed internal revenue laws, related statutes, and tax conventions,

children.label: B
children.text: specific law enforcement activities of the Internal Revenue Service, including specific compliance activities such as examinations, collection activities, and criminal investigations,

children.label: C
children.text: specific procurement activities of the Internal Revenue Service, or

children.label: D
children.text: except as provided in subsection (d)(3), specific personnel actions.

children.label: A
children.text: mission and objectives, and standards of performance relative to either, and

children.label: B
children.text: annual and long-range strategic plans.

children.label: A
children.text: plans for modernization of the tax system,

children.label: B
children.text: plans for outsourcing or managed competition, and

children.label: C
children.text: plans for training and education.

children.label: A
children.text: recommend to the President candidates for appointment as the Commissioner of Internal Revenue and recommend to the President the removal of the Commissioner;

children.label: B
children.text: review the Commissioner’s selection, evaluation, and compensation of Internal Revenue Service senior executives who have program management responsibility over significant functions of the Internal Revenue Service; and

children.label: C
children.text: review and approve the Commissioner’s plans for any major reorganization of the Internal Revenue Service.

children.label: A
children.text: review and approve the budget request of the Internal Revenue Service prepared by the Commissioner;

children.label: B
children.text: submit such budget request to the Secretary of the Treasury; and

children.label: C
children.text: ensure that the budget request supports the annual and long-range strategic plans.

children.heading: Taxpayer protection
children.label: 5 Taxpayer protection
children.text: To ensure the proper treatment of taxpayers by the employees of the Internal Revenue Service.

children.label: i
children.text: is described in subsection (b)(1)(A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: is described in subsection (b)(1)(D) and is not otherwise a Federal officer or employee,

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: B Chairperson
children.text: In lieu of the amount specified in subparagraph (A), the Chairperson of the Oversight Board shall be compensated at a rate of $50,000 per year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The members of the Oversight Board shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for employees of agencies under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, United States Code, to attend meetings of the Oversight Board and, with the advance approval of the Chairperson of the Oversight Board, while otherwise away from their homes or regular places of business for purposes of duties as a member of the Oversight Board.

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: The Oversight Board shall include in its annual report under subsection (f)(3)(A) information with respect to the travel expenses allowed for members of the Oversight Board under this paragraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Chairperson of the Oversight Board may appoint and terminate any personnel that may be necessary to enable the Board to perform its duties.

children.heading: Detail of Government employees
children.label: B Detail of Government employees
children.text: Upon request of the Chairperson of the Oversight Board, a Federal agency shall detail a Federal Government employee to the Oversight Board without reimbursement. Such detail shall be without interruption or loss of civil service status or privilege.

children.heading: Procurement of temporary and intermittent services
children.label: 4 Procurement of temporary and intermittent services
children.text: The Chairperson of the Oversight Board may procure temporary and intermittent services under , United States Code.section 3109(b) of title 5

children.heading: Term
children.label: A Term
children.text: The members of the Oversight Board shall elect for a 2-year term a chairperson from among the members appointed under subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.label: i
children.text: establishing committees;

children.label: ii
children.text: setting meeting places and times;

children.label: iii
children.text: establishing meeting agendas; and

children.label: iv
children.text: developing rules for the conduct of business.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 2 Meetings
children.text: The Oversight Board shall meet at least quarterly and at such other times as the Chairperson determines appropriate.

children.heading: Annual
children.label: A Annual
children.text: The Oversight Board shall each year report with respect to the conduct of its responsibilities under this title to the President, the Committees on Ways and Means, Government Reform and Oversight, and Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Committees on Finance, Governmental Affairs, and Appropriations of the Senate.

children.heading: Additional report
children.label: B Additional report
children.text: Upon a determination by the Oversight Board under subsection (c)(1)(B) that the organization and operation of the Internal Revenue Service are not allowing it to carry out its mission, the Oversight Board shall report such determination to the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate.
